steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The data shows mammal studies had a larger average effect than amphibian studies overall, yet some individual amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average. The correct answer must follow logically from that overlap.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — : if some amphibian studies exceeded the mammal average, they necessarily exceeded at least some individual mammal studies — this follows directly from the data. ✓
  • Choice B — : makes a claim about reporting practices that the data does not address. ✗
  • Choice C — : reverses the stated averages — the passage says mammals had the larger average, not amphibians. ✗
  • Choice D — : makes a specific claim about Shier's study that cannot be verified from the graph alone. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
